response:
**Here's a comprehensive set of pytest-style unit tests** for the `is_valid_email` function:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # replace with actual import


class TestIsValidEmail:
    
    # ==================== HAPPY PATH TESTS ====================
    
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("email", [
        "user@example.com",
        "user.name@example.com",
        "user_name123@example.com",
        "user+tag@example.com",
        "user%name@example.com",
        "user-name@example.com",
        "User.Name123@Example.com",
        "test.email+alex@sub.domain.co.uk",
        "very.common@example.com",
        "disposable.style.email.with+symbol@example.com",
        "other.email-with-hyphen@example.com",
        "x@example.com",  # minimal local part
        "a@b.co",         # minimal domain
    ])
    def test_valid_emails(self, email):
        assert is_valid_email(email) is True
    
    # ==================== EDGE CASES ====================
    
    def test_empty_string(self):
        assert is_valid_email("") is False
    
    def test_none_input(self):
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(None)
    
    def test_whitespace_only(self):
        assert is_valid_email("   ") is False
        assert is_valid_email(" ") is False
        assert is_valid_email("\t\n") is False
    
    def test_email_with_leading_trailing_whitespace(self):
        assert is_valid_email(" user@example.com ") is False
        assert is_valid_email("\tuser@example.com\n") is False
    
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("email", [
        "user@.com",                    # empty subdomain
        "user@domain.",                 # trailing dot
        "@example.com",                 # no local part
        "user@",                        # no domain
        "user@domain",                  # no TLD
        "user@domain.c",                # TLD too short (1 char)
        "user@domain..com",             # consecutive dots
        ".user@example.com",            # leading dot in local
        "user.@example.com",            # trailing dot in local
        "user..name@example.com",       # consecutive dots in local
    ])
    def test_invalid_formats(self, email):
        assert is_valid_email(email) is False
    
    # ==================== ERROR CASES ====================
    
    def test_non_string_input(self):
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(123)
        
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(3.14)
        
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(True)
        
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email([])
    
    # ==================== INTERNATIONAL & SPECIAL CASES ====================
    
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("email", [
        "user@sub.domain.com",
        "user@sub.sub.domain.com",
        "user.name+tag@sub.domain.co.uk",
        "test123@domain-name.com",
        "test@domain-name.co.uk",
    ])
    def test_complex_valid_domains(self, email):
        assert is_valid_email(email) is True
    
    def test_very_long_email(self):
        local_part = "a" * 64
        domain = "b" * 253 + ".com"
        email = f"{local_part}@{domain}"
        # Note: This regex doesn't enforce RFC length limits
        assert is_valid_email(email) is True
    
    def test_unicode_characters(self):
        # Current regex doesn't support internationalized email (IDN)
        assert is_valid_email("user@exämple.com") is False
        assert is_valid_email("üser@example.com") is False
        assert is_valid_email("user@例子.中国") is False
    
    # ==================== SPECIFIC REGEX BEHAVIOR TESTS ====================
    
    def test_percent_plus_minus_in_local_part(self):
        assert is_valid_email("user%name@example.com") is True
        assert is_valid_email("user+tag@example.com") is True
        assert is_valid_email("user-tag@example.com") is True
        assert is_valid_email("user.name@example.com") is True
    
    def test_numbers_in_domain(self):
        assert is_valid_email("user@123domain.com") is True
        assert is_valid_email("user@domain123.com") is True
        assert is_valid_email("user@sub1.sub2.domain.com") is True
```

---

### To run the tests:

```bash
pytest test_email_validator.py -v
```

### Key Testing Areas Covered:

1. **Happy Path**: Common valid email formats
2. **Edge Cases**: Empty strings, minimal lengths, boundary conditions
3. **Error Cases**: Type errors (non-string inputs)
4. **Format Violations**: Missing parts, invalid
